what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note that records Tim's agreement with and refinement of a design point: member decision requests should be returned as typed option-sets rendered as tap-targets, and should navigate to the relevant anchor for context.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares a note with id item-d9a85559, type note, state posted, title Tim · point, author tim, channel dragnet-development.
A links.commented_on entry points to board://item-bb66a021.
Body is tagged [point] and responds to a prior design point about structured questions.
It proposes typed option-sets / tap-targets instead of free-text questions.
It invokes the GitHub review requested / suggested change → accept loop as an analogy.
Tim adds that specific decisions should deep-link to the relevant place/anchor.
Created and updated timestamps are identical: 2026-06-24T09:52:07.907629+00:00.
standouts: Agreement that decision requests should be returned as structured, typed option-sets rendered as tap-targets.
Requirement to navigate the user to the specific anchor or context being decided on.
Use of the GitHub suggested-change accept loop as a generalized interaction model.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Markdown body uses a [point] tag to label the note kind.
Reply relationship is encoded via links.commented_on rather than inline prose.
concerns: The note's full meaning depends on the content of board://item-bb66a021, which is not included here.
No concrete schema or example of the proposed option-set / tap-target is provided.
Terms like member, overlay, and anchors are used without file-local definitions.
notes: A short design-consensus note from Tim agreeing that future decision requests should be structured choices with deep links to their anchors.
questions: What is the exact definition of a member and the overlay in this system?
What does anchor refer to here — a code location, a design artifact, or a UI element?
What is the content of item-bb66a021 that this note comments on?
Is there an existing or planned data schema for the typed option-sets?
Who or what generates the option-sets — a tool, an agent, or a human?
purpose_vs_actual: The frontmatter type and [point] tag frame it as a brief design point/comment, and the body does exactly that: it records agreement and a refinement, so purpose and content align.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish and record consensus that member-to-Tim decision requests should become actionable, structured choices that deep-link to their context, moving the workflow toward accept/reject-style interactions rather than prose parsing.
novelty: It generalizes the GitHub suggested-change accept loop to arbitrary member decision requests and couples it with anchor-based navigation so the decision maker can see the specific context before choosing.
contribution: It contributes a concrete interaction principle — decision requests as typed option-sets with tap-targets and anchor navigation — plus a recorded agreement from Tim that a fused system would preserve as a design constraint.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note in which Tim agrees that decision requests from members should be returned as typed option-sets rendered as tap-targets and should navigate to the relevant anchor, likening the loop to GitHub suggested-change acceptance.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate a design consensus for structured, context-linked decision interactions in the dragnet-development channel.
